delete.description:
Service for deleting PGP keys.

Uses script defined in b2bAPIs.properties, pgpKey.deleteScript, or ./pgp/delete.sh by default. This script takes two inputs: output file and key ID to delete. If the delete was successful, the output document should be empty (but still exist). If the delete failed, the document should contain a single line with the word “failed”.

200.description:
Service for deleting PGP keys.

Uses script defined in b2bAPIs.properties, pgpKey.deleteScript, or ./pgp/delete.sh by default. This script takes two inputs: output file and key ID to delete. If the delete was successful, the output document should be empty (but still exist). If the delete failed, the document should contain a single line with the word “failed”.

post.description:
Service for creating new PGP keys.

Uses the script defined in b2bAPIs.properties, pgpKey.createScript property, or ./pgp/create.sh by default. This script should take two inputs: the input file (which contains the data to import) and an output file. The output file should contain one line which has the key’s ID.  If the import was bad, the file should be empty, or contain an empty string (but it must still exist).

201.description:
Service for creating new PGP keys.

Uses the script defined in b2bAPIs.properties, pgpKey.createScript property, or ./pgp/create.sh by default. This script should take two inputs: the input file (which contains the data to import) and an output file. The output file should contain one line which has the key’s ID.  If the import was bad, the file should be empty, or contain an empty string (but it must still exist).

200.description:
Service for creating new PGP keys.

Uses the script defined in b2bAPIs.properties, pgpKey.createScript property, or ./pgp/create.sh by default. This script should take two inputs: the input file (which contains the data to import) and an output file. The output file should contain one line which has the key’s ID.  If the import was bad, the file should be empty, or contain an empty string (but it must still exist).

get.description:
Service for reading PGP keys.

For listing: Uses script defined in b2bAPIs.properties, pgpKey.readScript, or ./pgp/read.sh by default,  if a key ID is not specified. This script takes one input: the output file.  The output file should be formatted like:

<Key 1 ID>

<Key 1 UID>

<Key 2 ID>

<Key 2 UID>

Etc…

 

For Single Reads: Uses script defined in b2bAPIs.properties, pgpKey.exportScript, or./pgp/export.sh by default, if a key ID is specified. This script takes two inputs: the output file and the keyID. The output file should be formatted so that the first line is the key ID, the second line is the UID and the rest of the file is the key data:

 

<Key ID>

<Key UID>

<Key Data

Key Data

Key Data…>

The output file should be formatted so that the first line is the key ID, the second line is the UID and the rest of the file is the key data:

name.description: The name of the workflow. Cannot contain spaces.

description.description: The description of the workflow. Can be different for each version.

category.description: Optional category name that creates the category. Does not affect processing.

commitStepsUponError.description: Only used when the Enable Transaction field is set. If true, all steps up to an error are committed.

deadlineHours.description: The number of hours by which the business process must complete once it is put in a queue, in combination with the number of minutes.

deadlineMinutes.description: The number of minutes by which the business process must complete once it is put in a queue, in combination with the number of hours.

documentStorage.description: Allows for the selection of the document storage type for the document payload data.

documentTracking.description: If true, documents in this process model are trackable according to your document tracking settings.

enableTransaction.description: Treats the entire process as a single transaction if true. Thus, either all steps complete, or none of them do.

eventReportingLevel.description: Determines the level of events to generate for this process.

firstNotificationHours.description: Enables you to specify whether to receive notification before a business process deadline (as long as business process remains in a queue), in combination with the number of minutes.

firstNotificationMinutes.description: Enables you to specify whether to receive notification before a business process deadline (as long as business process remains in a queue), in combination with the number of hours.

lifespanHours.description: Number of hours in the lifespan of the workflow. If not set, uses system default (2 days).

lifespanDays.description: Number of days in the lifespan of the workflow. If not set, uses system default (2 days).

node.description: Only valid in a cluster: Select the node on which you want the process to run.

nodePreference.description: Only valid in a cluster: Specify whether the process must run on the selected node or can use others.

onfaultProcessing.description: If true, on-fault activity specified in the business process executes immediately in the event of a system error.

persistenceLevel.description: Enables you to specify, at the business process level, the types of data to retain in the database.

queue.description: Defines the queue level the system uses to allocate resources for performance optimizations.

recoveryLevel.description: Allows the selection of the level of recovery for this business process if it is interrupted during execution.

removalMethod.description: Determines if expired data is archived or purged. Archived data is stored in your file system.It is necessary to select one of the value, otherwise may not be eligible for archive or purge

secondNotificationHours.description: Enables you to specify whether to receive another notification before a business process deadline (as long as business process remains in a queue), in combination with the number of minutes.

secondNotificationMinutes.description: Enables you to specify whether to receive another notification before a business process deadline (as long as business process remains in a queue), in combination with the number of hours.

softstopRecoveryLevel.description: In the event of a softstop, determines how the business process should resume.

useBPQueuing.description: If true, the business process is placed in a queue for processing. This is the recommended value.
